PARAM.name: <T>

PARAM.description: the type that the factory creates

PARAM.name: prototype
PARAM.content: the object to clone each time in the factory
PARAM.description: the object to clone each time in the factory

RETURN.description: the prototype factory, or a ConstantFactory#NULL_INSTANCE if the prototype is null
RETURN.content: the prototype factory, or a ConstantFactory#NULL_INSTANCE if the prototype is null

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException

PARAM.name: <T>
PARAM.content: the type that the factory creates
PARAM.description: the type that the factory creates

PARAM.name: classToInstantiate
PARAM.content: the Class to instantiate each time in the factory
PARAM.description: the Class to instantiate each time in the factory

RETURN.description: the Class to instantiate each time in the factory.
RETURN.content: the Class to instantiate each time in the factory.

PARAM.description: the Class to instantiate each time in the factory, not null
PARAM.name: classToInstantiate
PARAM.content: the Class to instantiate each time in the factory

PARAM.description: parameter types for the constructor, can be null
PARAM.name: paramTypes
PARAM.content: parameter types for the constructor, can be null

PARAM.description: the arguments to pass to the constructor, can be null
PARAM.name: args
PARAM.content: the arguments to pass to the constructor, can be null

PARAM.assertion: assert (paramTypes != null && args != null) ? !Arrays.deepEquals(paramTypes, new Object[] {args}) : true;
PARAM.name: paramTypes and args

RETURN.description: the Factory that can create objects of a specific type using the arguments specified to this method.
RETURN.content: the Factory that can create objects of a specific type using the arguments specified to this method.

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if the paramTypes and args don't match
THROWS.description: if the paramTypes and args don't match
